name: ringbuffer
description: A fixed-capacity, generic ring (circular) buffer. Push appends and overwrites the oldest element once full; Pop removes the oldest (FIFO), Len reports the current count, and ToSlice returns the elements oldest-to-newest as a fresh copy. Stdlib only, go 1.23, generic over any element type. Verified: gofmt-clean, go build/vet/test all green.

maintain.request: Add a non-destructive Peek() (T, error) method that returns the oldest element without removing it (returning ErrEmpty when empty), plus a Full() bool helper that reports whether the next Push will overwrite the oldest element. Include table-driven tests for both.

review.bug: The Push full-check uses the wrong boundary, comparing r.count to len(r.buf)-1 instead of len(r.buf), so the buffer starts overwriting (and advancing head) one element too early and silently drops an item before it is actually full.
keywords: compares count to len(r.buf)-1 instead of len(r.buf)
premature overwrite advances head too early
drops the oldest element before full
